stallions.name: plan-architect
stallions.description: Planning specialist. Asks probing questions, explores the codebase, and generates phased implementation plans with [PARALLEL]/[SEQUENTIAL] tags and a ready-to-use TODO.md.
triggerOn: new feature request
refactor or restructuring
UI/UX change with broader implications
vague feature request needing clarification

stallions.name: plan-implementer
stallions.description: Execution specialist. Reads a plan, proposes solo-vs-agent-team approach, tracks TODO checkboxes, and coordinates parallel work across agent teams.
triggerOn: user has a plan ready and wants to implement it
plan has [PARALLEL] tasks that could run concurrently
continuing implementation from a previous phase

skills.name: ui-design
skills.description: Builds frontend UI in a specific personal design language — intentional, anti-template, semantically grounded. Interrogates product domain, user type, and mode before generating any code.
triggerOn: building web components, pages, marketing sites, dashboards, or app screens
user asks for UI that doesn't look AI-generated or vibe-coded

skills.name: shadcn
skills.description: Manages shadcn/ui components: adding, searching, fixing, debugging, styling, and composing. Provides project context, component docs, and usage examples.
triggerOn: working with shadcn/ui, component registries, or presets
project has a components.json file
shadcn init or component-level work

skills.name: tdd
skills.description: Test-driven development workflow: deep-module design, mocking strategy, interface design before implementation, refactoring patterns.
triggerOn: writing tests for a new module
setting up a TDD workflow
refactoring with test coverage as a constraint

skills.name: to-issues
skills.description: Converts a conversation or plan into discrete GitHub issues and publishes them to the project issue tracker.
triggerOn: user wants to create GitHub issues from the current context
breaking down a plan into trackable work items

skills.name: to-prd
skills.description: Synthesizes a structured PRD from the current conversation context — problem statement, solution, user stories, implementation decisions, testing decisions — and publishes it to the issue tracker.
triggerOn: user wants to create a PRD from the current conversation
formalizing a feature discussion into a product document

skills.name: triage
skills.description: Triages incoming issues: labels, prioritizes, routes, and applies triage vocabulary to keep the backlog clean.
triggerOn: triaging a GitHub issue
labeling or routing new issues
running a triage session on the backlog

skills.name: web-design-guidelines
skills.description: Reviews UI code against the latest Web Interface Guidelines. Fetches live rules from the Vercel source URL, then audits specified files for compliance, accessibility, and UX best practices.
triggerOn: reviewing UI code for quality or accessibility
auditing design against best practices
user asks to review my UI or check my site
